Appearances: John Golden for Vera May (Executor).
Jim Thompson for Irene Tropiec (Former Executor).

The Fifth Amended First and Final Report of Successor Executor, etc. is under submission.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Feb-17-2015 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Nov-18-2014 Continued To Feb-17-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sarah Sheppard appearing for Jeff Low on behalf of Carlos Tropiec (Personal Representative of Irene Tropiec). John Golden for Vera May (Successor Executor).

Mr. Golden stated that they have successfully mediated the case and settled it, that they need to provide a notice of proposed action to Nyna Van Atta and that they need to file a petition to approve the settlement. The Petition for Instructions was continued to February 17, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on setting. Mr. Golden also stated that he anticipates having a petition for distribution filed by then.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Sep-16-2014 Continued To Nov-18-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Jun-03-2014 Continued To Sep-16-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Mar-18-2014 Continued To Jun-03-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Breckenridge for Carlos Tropiec (Beneficiary). John Golden for Vera May (Executor).

Mr. Golden stated that the deceased Executor, Irene Tropiec, filed the 850 petition and that they were getting ready for trial when she died in December. Mr. Golden requested that the Court continue the matter to allow time for a probate to be opened up for Irene Tropiec and to get Carlos in place. At Counsel's request, the Court continued the matter to June 3, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Golden stated that an amended petition would be filed and served.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Feb-18-2014 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Feb-18-2014 Continued To Mar-18-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Jan-14-2014 Continued To Feb-18-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearance: James Thompson for Irene Tropiec (Executor). John Golden for Vera May (Respondent).

Mr. Thompson reported that Irene Tripiec is deceased, that he does not know about her estate, that she resided in San Mateo, that she has son, Carlos who is not in court and that he will need time to sort things out and petition to appoint a successor. The matter was continued to February 18, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The petition to appoint a successor can be set on the same date. The Court will order the filing of a final account when a successor is appointed.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Oct-01-2013 Continued To Jan-14-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Thompson for Executor. John Golden for Vera May (Respondent).

Mr. Golden stated that settlement has broken down, that Mr. Thompson's Client provided an accounting, that it will be filed and that he intends to object to the accounting. Mr. Golden also asked to set a trial date sixty days out and stated that they still need to do discovery. The Petition for Instructions was continued to January 14, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status. The accounting petition is to have the same hearing date of January 14, 2014.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Aug-21-2013 Continued To Oct-01-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Thompson for Irene Tropiec (Executor). John Golden for Vera May (Respondent/Heir).

Mr. Golden stated that they are in settlement discussions and requested a 30-day continuance. The Petition for Instructions was continued to October 1, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on settlement discussions and trial setting. Mr. Thompson advised the Court that Nyna Van Atta an Heir who is entitled to a 1/3 interest has not participated in settlement discussions, has not hired an Attorney and has not filed anything. Mr. Thompson also stated that he will provide Nyna with notice of the October 1, 2013 hearing date.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Loos. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Receipts For The Blocked Accounts
Off Calendar Oct-29-2012 Off Calendar

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (431-433 Second Avenue, San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar Sep-17-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Thompson for Irene Tropiec (Executor).

The sale was confirmed at $1,200,000.00 for 100% of the property. The Estate owns 75% of the property. The sale was based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed on August 20, 2012. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review and receipt of the order). The proceeds of the sale are to be placed into blocked accounts. Status on filing of the receipts for the blocked accounts was set for Ocotber 29, 2012.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (431-433 Second Ave. S.F.)
Off Calendar Jul-23-2012 Off Calendar

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (431-433 Second Ave. S.F.)
Off Calendar Jul-02-2012 Continued To Jul-23-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jim Thompson for Irene Tropiec (Executor).

Counsel informed the Court that the Buyer for the San Francisco Property is not in court. The Court questioned if notice was given to the Purchaser. Counsel reported that notice was faxed to the broker for the Purchaser. The Court indicated that fax notice is not acceptable absent an agreement.

The Court passed the sale of the San Francisco Property and proceeded with the sale of the Sacramento Property. The sale of 3040-3050 23rd Avenue, Sacramento (listed on the calendar as San Francisco in error) was confirmed at $90,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale and a copy of the Reappraisal submitted this morning. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property and allowed 5% commission (subject to review of the order). Bond is not required.

Case recalled at 9:54 a.m., The Sale of 431-433 Second Avenue, San Francisco was continued to July 23, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow Counsel to clear up notice issue. The Court raised concern with the Executor's 25% claim of the property.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (3040-3050 23rd Ave. S.F.)
Off Calendar Jul-02-2012 Granted Per Written Order
